popularStory.headline: History of Elizabeth Stewart (1362-1446)
popularStory.description: Elizabeth Stewart, Princess of Scotland was a daughter of Robert II of Scotland and Euphemia de Ross. She was born between 1356 and 1370, well after her parents' marriage on 2 May 1355.[1] Her brother …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Stewart Princess of Scotland於1346年出生在蘇格蘭艾斯爾登東納爾德時，她的父親Robert II Stewart King of Scotland30歲，母親Elizabeth Mure of Rowallan26歲。她於1372年11月13日與Sir Thomas de la Haye 7th Lord of Erroll在蘇格蘭艾斯爾登東納爾德結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1389年在英國蘇格蘭培爾培斯享年43歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭培爾埃羅爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Stewart Princess of Scotland于1346年出生在苏格兰艾斯爾登東納爾德时，她的父亲Robert II Stewart King of Scotland30岁，母亲Elizabeth Mure of Rowallan26岁。她于1372年11月13日与Sir Thomas de la Haye 7th Lord of Erroll在苏格兰艾斯爾登東納爾德结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1389年在英国苏格兰培爾培斯享年43岁，埋葬在苏格兰培爾埃羅爾。

nameMeanings.name: Stewart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: originally an occupational name for an administrative official of an estate, from Middle English stiward, Old English stigweard, stīweard, a compound of stig ‘house(hold)’ + weard ‘guardian’. In the Anglo-Saxon period this title was used of an officer controlling the domestic affairs of a household, especially of the royal household; after the Norman Conquest it was also used more widely as the native equivalent of Seneschal, for the steward of a manor or manager of an estate. In Scotland the term was also used of a magistrate originally appointed by the king to administer crown lands, forming a stewartry.

History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland, the royal family of Scotland from the 14th century, and of England from 1603, when James VI of Scotland acceded to the English throne as James I. There were many minor branches of the family left in Britain after the flight of James II in 1688, but not every bearer of the surname can claim relationship with the royal house, even in Scotland. Every great house in medieval England and Scotland had its steward, and in many cases the office gave rise to a hereditary surname. The fall of the house of Stuart in Britain, conversely, led to the establishment of several highly placed branches bearing this surname in continental Europe, which are in most cases related to the old Scottish royal family.

variants.name: Stuart
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire and Banffshire): variant of   Stewart  . This French form of the name, there being no W in the French language, was adopted by Mary, Queen of Scots, on her return to Scotland in 1561 and has since come to be more popular among royalists than the earlier spellings.
History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland (see note at   Stewart  ).

variants.name: Stewardson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Westmorland): patronymic from Middle English stiward ‘steward’ (see Stewart ) + -son ‘son of the steward’.

variants.name: Steward
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Stewart  .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Stewart Princess of Scotland was born in 1346, in Dundonald, Ayrshire, Scotland, her father, Robert II Stewart King of Scotland, was 30 and her mother, Elizabeth Mure of Rowallan, was 26. She married Sir Thomas de la Haye 7th Lord of Erroll on 13 November 1372, in Dundonald, Ayrshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1389, in Perth, Perthshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 43, and was buried in Errol, Perthshire, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Stewart Princess of Scotland est née en 1346, à Dundonald, Ayrshire, Écosse, son père, Robert II Stewart King of Scotland, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Mure of Rowallan, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Thomas de la Haye 7th Lord of Erroll le 13 novembre 1372, à Dundonald, Ayrshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1389, à Perth, Perthshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 43 ans, et a été enterrée à Errol, Perthshire, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Stewart Princess of Scotland 1346 in Dundonald, Ayrshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Robert II Stewart King of Scotland 30 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth Mure of Rowallan 26. Sie hat am 13. November 1372 in Dundonald, Ayrshire, Scotland Sir Thomas de la Haye 7th Lord of Erroll geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1389 im Alter von 43 Jahren in Perth, Perthshire, Schottland, United Kingdom und wurde in Errol, Perthshire, Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Stewart Princess of Scotland è nata il nel 1346 nel Dundonald, Ayrshire, Scotland, sua padre, Robert II Stewart King of Scotland, aveva 30 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth Mure of Rowallan, aveva 26 anni. Lei sposato Sir Thomas de la Haye 7th Lord of Erroll il 13 novembre 1372, nel Dundonald, Ayrshire, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1389, nel Perth, Perthshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, all'età di 43 anni, e fu sepolta a Errol, Perthshire, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Stewart Princess of Scotlandが1346年にスコットランドエイルシュアイアダンドナルドで生まれた時，父Robert II Stewart King of Scotlandは30歳，母Elizabeth Mure of Rowallanは26歳であった。1372年11月13日にスコットランドエイルシュアイアダンドナルドでSir Thomas de la Haye 7th Lord of Errollと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1389年にイギリススコットランドペルスシュアイアペルスで43歳で死去し，スコットランドペルスシュアイアエロルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Stewart Princess of Scotland(이)가 1346년 스컷랜드 에이르실르 던도널드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert II Stewart King of Scotland(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Mure of Rowallan(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 1372년 11월 13일 스컷랜드 에이르실르 던도널드에서 Sir Thomas de la Haye 7th Lord of Erroll(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1389년 영국 스코틀랜드 퍼스실르 퍼스에서 43세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 퍼스실르 에럴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Stewart Princess of Scotland nasceu em 1346, em Dundonald, Ayrshire, Scotland, seu pai, Robert II Stewart King of Scotland, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Mure of Rowallan, tinha 26 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Thomas de la Haye 7th Lord of Erroll em 13 de novembro de 1372, em Dundonald, Ayrshire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1389, em Perth, Perthshire, Escócia, United Kingdom, com 43 anos, e foi sepultada em Errol, Perthshire, Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Stewart Princess of Scotland родилась в 1346 г. в местности дундональд, аиршир, Шотландия, её отцу по имени Robert II Stewart King of Scotland было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Mure of Rowallan, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Thomas de la Haye 7th Lord of Erroll 13 ноября 1372 г. в местности дундональд, аиршир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1389 г. в местности пирт, пиртшир, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 43 лет и была похоронена в местности эррол, пиртшир, Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Stewart Princess of Scotland nació en 1346, en Dundonald, Ayrshire, Scotland, su papá, Robert II Stewart King of Scotland, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Mure of Rowallan, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Sir Thomas de la Haye 7th Lord of Erroll el 13 de noviembre de 1372, en Dundonald, Ayrshire, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1389, en Perth, Perthshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 43 años, y fue sepultada en Errol, Perthshire, Scotland.
